methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Mind Robotics, a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by RJ Scaringe, has raised over $1.0B to date. The valuation landscape includes one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

Given the stage and market context, a peer-median approach is not applicable; the comparable set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage companies with similar valuation tiers to support reliable cross-sectional analysis. Instead, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable framework. Using the $1.0B total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, this method implies a valuation of $2.0B.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.7B (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $2.0B. This range reflects the step-up multiple applied to the capital raised proxy, adjusted for typical variance in comparable transitions. Key uncertainties include the absence of sufficient narrow-peer comparables, which limits cross-validation of the stage-progression result, and the limited public-market representation in this sector. Investors should factor company-specific unit economics, capital efficiency, and competitive positioning—dimensions not captured in this quantitative framework—into final valuation decisions.
